text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 14 May 2021 

Dr Priya Nair
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Priya Nair
Consultant Neurologist,  

Daniel Harper, DOB: 14-02-1992, Hospital No: KCH1029384 NHS No. 9462753108  
Flat 3, 28 Rowan Close, London, SE15 3GH

Thank you for referring this gentleman, whom I reviewed today in clinic. He works as a gardener/groundskeeper, largely outdoors, and notes that heat and prolonged exertion vary seasonally and can leave him fatigued at the end of longer summer shifts. He reports that sleep may be fragmented after particularly hot days.

Regarding his events: His initial event was in March 2021 in Germany, arising from sleep. He awoke with jerking of the left arm and facial twitching. A second event occurred in Italy the following May 2021, once more during the night, lasting four minutes with similar semiology. Since then, he has not described clear further nocturnal focal features; however, his partner has noticed occasional brief morning myoclonic jerks affecting both arms on days after poor sleep or strenuous late work, and he recounts rare, very brief lapses in awareness shortly after waking. There is no history of prolonged convulsive activity, tongue biting, or incontinence reported to date.

On balance, the combination of early-morning myoclonic jerks and brief lapses in awareness, along with normal interictal function, is most in keeping with a generalised epilepsy. I have explained to him that, based on the history, he has generalised epilepsy, and we will investigate further to support this impression. The two earlier nocturnal events with lateralised motor signs may represent focal features at onset within a generalised epilepsy syndrome or could reflect sleep-related activation; we will clarify this with investigations.

Examination today was unremarkable with no lateralising neurological signs. He denies alcohol or recreational drug use. He reports regular hydration at work but acknowledges that hot weather and extended shifts can curtail sleep and increase morning jerks.

Plan:
- Arrange routine and sleep-deprived EEG to assess for generalised spike–wave or polyspike–wave activity.
- Brain MRI (epilepsy protocol) to exclude a structural substrate, given the initial lateralised semiology during sleep.
- Safety advice provided regarding swimming unsupervised, working at heights, and the potential impact of sleep deprivation and heat; he will modify shifts during heatwaves where possible and maintain good hydration.
- We discussed anti-seizure medication options suitable for generalised epilepsy; he prefers to defer treatment until investigations are complete, provided events remain brief and infrequent. He will contact us urgently if he has any convulsive seizure or escalation in frequency.
- Driving counselling given as per DVLA guidance; he understands the legal requirements and will refrain from driving pending clarification and stability.

I will review him after EEG and MRI to confirm diagnosis and agree on treatment. Please let me know if there are any concerns in the interim.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: 1) Identify explicitly dated seizure events: The letter states there was an initial event in March 2021 and a second event in May 2021. 2) Count events: 2 events. 3) Determine observation window: From March 2021 to May 2021 is approximately 2 months. 4) Other events (morning myoclonic jerks and brief lapses) are described as occasional/rare without numeric counts or a defined time unit, so they cannot be converted into a frequency per the allowed formats. 5) Prioritizing explicit numeric expressions, the quantifiable frequency over the observed period is 2 events in ~2 months → 2 per 2 month.
